---
title: Customizing theme
description: Modify colors, typography, and other design tokens to match your brand.
---

Customize your theme to align with your brand identity. The theming system applies across all design system components and styles in your project.

## Accessing theme settings

To edit your theme:

1. Click the **Components** tab in the top navigation
2. Select **Theme** from the left sidebar
3. The theme editor shows all your design tokens organized by category

<img src="https://developers.elementor.com/docs/assets/img/elementor-placeholder-image.png" alt="The Subframe interface showing the Components tab selected in the top navigation, with Theme highlighted in the left sidebar, and the theme editor panel open on the right" />

## Quick theme updates

For fast customization, use the **Edit theme** button to apply pre-selected theme colors and styles. These options provide professionally designed color palettes that you can apply instantly.

1. Click **Edit theme** in the theme editor
2. Browse available theme styles
3. Select one—changes apply automatically to your entire project

## Customizing color tokens

### Editing existing colors

Click any color token to open the color picker. Changes save automatically and apply to all components using that token.

Your theme includes several color palettes:

- **Brand colors** — Your primary brand color in shades from 50 to 900
- **Neutral colors** — Grays for text, borders, and backgrounds
- **Semantic colors** — Error (red), Warning (yellow), Success (green)
- **Utility colors** — Default font, background, and border colors

### Creating custom color tokens

Add new color tokens for specific use cases:

1. Navigate to **Components > Theme**
2. Click the **+** button near existing color tokens
3. Name your token (use descriptive names like `accent-purple` or `surface-elevated`)
4. Set the value by entering a hex code or referencing an existing token

<Warning>
When you delete a custom token, all references to it convert to the hex code value. This breaks the connection to your theme, so components won't update when you change colors.
</Warning>

## Typography tokens

Customize fonts, sizes, and text styles:

**Font families** — Choose from Google Fonts or use custom fonts. Subframe handles font loading automatically.

**Font sizes** — Adjust heading and body text sizes. Each size includes line height, font weight, and letter spacing.

**Text styles** — Modify the complete style for headings (heading-1, heading-2, heading-3), body text, captions, and code.

Changes to typography tokens update all text throughout your project.

## Spacing, borders, and shadows

**Spacing** — Includes Tailwind's default spacing scale (0-96) plus any custom values you add. Use these for padding, margins, and gaps.

**Border radius** — Define corner roundness with presets: sm (4px), md (8px), lg (12px), or full (fully rounded).

**Shadows** — Choose from elevation presets: sm, md, lg, and overlay. Each shadow preset can include multiple layers for complex effects.

## Syncing theme changes to code

After updating your theme, sync the changes to your codebase:

```bash
npx @subframe/cli sync --all
```

This updates your `tailwind.config.js` or `theme.css` file with the new token values. All components in your codebase will automatically use the updated theme.

## Best practices

### Use semantic names

Name custom tokens based on their purpose, not their appearance. Use `accent-color` instead of `purple-500`, so you can change the color later without renaming.

### Reference existing tokens

When creating new tokens, reference existing ones when possible. This maintains relationships between colors and makes theme-wide updates easier.

### Test across components

After making significant theme changes, review your components to ensure everything looks correct. Some color combinations may need adjustment for proper contrast.
